bird.name: 红翎毛翅燕
bird.summary: 红翎毛翅燕（學名：Stelgidopteryx ruficollis，葡萄牙語：Andorinha-serradora-do-sul，西班牙語：Golondrina gorjirrufa，台湾作南方紅翎粗翅燕）是雀形目燕科毛翅燕屬下的一種鳥類。這是一種中小型的燕子，主要分佈於中…

locations.name: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约
locations.summary: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约（法語：Louis Pierre Vieillot，1748年5月10日—1830年8月24日）是法国的鳥類學家。 维埃约是第一批用卡尔·林奈式二名法科学描述鸟类的研究者之一。至少有26个他命名的鸟类至今仍在学术界使用…

locations.name: 布宜诺斯艾利斯省
locations.summary: 布宜诺斯艾利斯省（西班牙語：Buenos Aires）為南美國家阿根廷二十三省之一，是阿根廷二十四个自治省份（estados autogobernados）之一或者一级司法管辖权（jurisdicciones de primer orde…

locations.name: 圭亚那 (地区)
locations.summary: 圭亚那（西班牙語：Las Guayanas；英語：The Guianas；法語：Guyanes；荷蘭語：Guyana's；葡萄牙語：Guianas）是南美洲北部、西北部的一个地区，并被划分为了数个主权国家和区域。前哥伦布时期，圭亚那地区的…

relations.subject: 红翎毛翅燕
relations.evidence: |year=2020 |title=Southern Rough-winged Swallow Stelgidopteryx ruficollis |volume=2020 |article-number=e.T22712162A137675816 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2020-3.RLTS.T22712162A137675816.en |access-date=20 January 2026}} | genus = Stelgidopteryx | species = ruficollis | authority = (Vieillot, 1817) | range_map = Stelgidopteryx ruficollis map.svg }} The southern rough-winged swallow (Stelgidopteryx ruficollis) a species of bird in the family Hirundinidae, the swallows and martins. It is found in Central America from Honduras south, in every mainland South American country except Chile, on Trinidad, and as a vagrant to Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Tobago, and the Falkland Islands.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

relations.subject: 红翎毛翅燕
relations.evidence: ruficollis: from southeastern Colombia south through eastern Ecuador, eastern Peru, and eastern Bolivia into northern and northeastern Argentina as far as Buenos Aires Province; from there east across southern and eastern Venezuela, the Guianas, and all of Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay The species has been documented as a vagrant on the Caribbean islands of Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, and Tobago that are arrayed along the north coast of South America, and also far to the southeast on the Falkland Islands. The southern rough-winged swallow inhabits a variety of open landscapes, especially those that have water features
